summary: Comprehensive testing of Product Alert Email feature. All 21 backend API tests pass (100%). Frontend UI tests pass - page loads with DashboardLayout, create alert form works with category dropdown and score slider, subscriptions list shows with toggle and delete actions, ProGate blocks free users.
seed_data_creation: Created test subscriptions via API during testing (prefixed with TEST_), cleaned up after tests
context_for_next_testing_agent: Product Alerts feature fully functional. All CRUD operations work: GET /api/product-alerts/subscriptions, POST create, PUT update, PUT toggle, DELETE. Categories endpoint returns 17 categories from products collection. History endpoint returns sent alerts (currently empty). Frontend at /product-alerts shows DashboardLayout with create form, subscriptions list with toggle/delete, and alert history section. ProGate blocks free users (403). Existing subscription for reviewer@trendscout.click: id=cj-ingestion-preview (Beauty & Electronics, 60+ score, currently inactive).

backend_api_tests.POST /api/product-alerts/subscriptions (: PASS - Creates subscription with id, categories, min_score, label, active=true
backend_api_tests.Create → GET persistence: PASS - Created subscription appears in list
backend_api_tests.Delete → GET removal: PASS - Deleted subscription removed from list

frontend_ui_tests.Category selection: PASS - Clicking category adds to selection, shows badge
frontend_ui_tests.Form submission: PASS - Creates alert, shows toast, closes form
frontend_ui_tests.Alert history section: PASS - Shows 'Recent Alerts Sent' with empty state

features_verified: GET /api/product-alerts/subscriptions - list user's alert subscriptions (requires auth + paid plan)
POST /api/product-alerts/subscriptions - create new subscription with categories and min_score
Create alert form opens with category dropdown, score slider, and submit
Subscription list shows with toggle and delete actions
ProGate blocks free users from accessing the feature (403 for unauthenticated)

mocked_apis.Resend email: RESEND_API_KEY is set but emails return 'skipped' status if not configured - this is expected and handled gracefully
